pi_items.source: Can I Use (https://caniuse.com/css-container-queries) - Full support starts at Chrome 107; Chrome 105 had partial support
Can I Use (https://caniuse.com/css-container-queries) - Firefox 110 is the first version with full support
Can I Use (https://caniuse.com/css-container-queries) - Full support starts at Safari 16.6; Safari 16 had partial/no support
Can I Use (https://caniuse.com/css-has) - Chrome 105 is first version with full support
Can I Use (https://caniuse.com/css-has) - Firefox 121 is first version with full support
Can I Use (https://caniuse.com/css-has) - Safari 15.4 is first version with support
Can I Use (https://caniuse.com/css-subgrid) - Chrome 117 is first version with full support
Can I Use (https://caniuse.com/css-subgrid) - Firefox 71 is first version with support (December 2019)
Can I Use (https://caniuse.com/css-subgrid) - Safari 16 is first version with support
Can I Use (https://caniuse.com/mdn-css_types_color_color-mix) - Chrome 111 is first version with support
Can I Use (https://caniuse.com/mdn-css_types_color_color-mix) - Firefox 113 is first version with support
Can I Use (https://caniuse.com/mdn-css_types_color_color-mix) - Safari 16.2 is first version with support
Can I Use (https://caniuse.com/css-nesting) - Chrome 112-119 had only partial support (required & prefix); Chrome 120 is first version with full support and relaxed parsing
Can I Use (https://caniuse.com/css-nesting) - Firefox 117 is first version with full support
Can I Use (https://caniuse.com/css-nesting) - Safari 16.5-17.1 had only partial support (required & prefix); Safari 17.2 is first version with full support and relaxed parsing
These are all legitimate authoritative sources for CSS documentation (MDN, Can I Use, W3C, CSSWG)

privileged_info_summary: Container Queries first supported in Chrome 107 (not 105), Firefox 110, and Safari 16.6 (not 16). The :has() pseudo-class first supported in Chrome 105, Firefox 121, and Safari 15.4. CSS Subgrid first supported in Chrome 117, Firefox 71, and Safari 16. The color-mix() function first supported in Chrome 111, Firefox 113, and Safari 16.2. CSS Nesting first supported with full support in Chrome 120 (partial support in 112-119), Firefox 117, and Safari 17.2 (partial support in 16.5-17.1). The task includes a version tolerance of 10, which means values can be off by up to 10 major versions. Required research sources are MDN, Can I Use, W3C, and CSSWG drafts.
